reasons: Franco's suppression is one of the cleanest ways to stop Cici's moving damage window.
Cici can keep attacking through many ordinary interruptions, so hard single-target suppression is especially valuable.

proof.summary: Cici wants to keep moving during Yo-Yo Blitz, while Franco can suppress her and create a burst window where she cannot freely kite.
worksBestWhen: Franco waits until Cici commits forward.
The team follows immediately during suppression.
Cici has limited terrain or movement space.
failureCases: Franco misses hook or suppresses without damage follow-up.
Cici keeps distance and baits Franco's cooldowns.
Franco targets Cici while her team can instantly peel.

reasons: Kaja can lock Cici during her mobility pattern and drag her away from safe skirmish spacing.
This works because Cici's strength is continuous movement, not burst immunity.

proof.summary: Cici wants to extend fights with movement and spell vamp, while Kaja can suppress and reposition her into a team burst zone.
worksBestWhen: Kaja waits for Cici to use Buoyant Bounce aggressively.
Kaja's team can burst Cici before she rebuilds distance.
Cici has no immediate peel behind her.
failureCases: Cici stays behind a frontline and avoids Kaja's range.
Kaja is forced to use suppression defensively before Cici commits.

reasons: Cici's Delight stacks and Yo-Yo Blitz skirmishing rely heavily on spell-vamp value.
Baxia can reduce healing received, making Cici's extended trades less reliable.

proof.summary: Cici wants to heal through long trades, while Baxia reduces healing received and makes her sustain-based fighting pattern easier to outvalue.
worksBestWhen: Baxia stays close enough to keep anti-heal pressure active.
The team avoids chasing Cici alone through long terrain.
Baxia's allies add burst or control while Cici's healing is reduced.
failureCases: Cici kites Baxia without committing to extended trades.
Baxia cannot reach her because of peel or terrain.
Cici disengages until Baxia's pressure window ends.

reasons: Alpha can fight Cici in longer trades because his kit provides repeated true-damage pressure and his own sustain.
Alpha can pressure Cici through extended trades because his repeated true-damage output helps bypass ordinary durability and directly contests Cici's sustain-based skirmish pattern.

proof.summary: Cici wants to kite and sustain through extended trades, while Alpha can keep pressure with repeated true damage from Beta and contest her in a direct fighter matchup.
worksBestWhen: Alpha can keep Cici within skill range.
Alpha lands repeated skill rotations instead of chasing blindly.
Anti-heal or team follow-up is available to reduce Cici's spell-vamp value.
failureCases: Cici keeps spacing and forces Alpha to miss skill rotations.
Cici gets peel or extra movement space from terrain.

reasons: Karrie can pressure Cici with repeated attacks that trigger true-damage marks during extended fights.
This works best with protection because Cici can still threaten Karrie if she reaches close range.

proof.summary: Cici wants extended sustain fights, while Karrie can pressure her with repeated true-damage marks if Karrie has enough space to attack safely.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has frontline or support protection.
Cici has already used mobility aggressively.
Karrie can keep attacking long enough to trigger Lightwheel Mark.
failureCases: Cici reaches Karrie before Karrie stacks damage.
Cici disengages before repeated attacks convert.
